title: Closures
description: Functions remember where they were born.

options: A function bundled with the variables from its outer scope

questions.explanation: Inner closes over outer's x (10), not the global.
var is function-scoped — all timeouts see the SAME i (3 at fire time).
let creates a NEW binding per iteration — each timeout captures its own i.
Two closures share the same `secret` — private state pattern.
Data hiding, currying, and memoization — closures do it all.
